positive_description: Subtle cranial and forehead depth adjustment. The forehead gains marginal forward projection, the cranial vault deepens slightly in the anterior-posterior direction, and the face may read as having a slightly more prominent brow shelf. The temples fill out subtly.
negative_description: The forehead flattens in the anterior-posterior axis, the cranial vault becomes shallower, and the temples become marginally more concave. The face reads as having a slightly flatter, more recessed upper structure.
symmetry_notes: Symmetric. Both 3/4 views confirm consistent bilateral behavior.
artifact_notes: Subtle component. No artifacts visible at any tested value. The visual change is most noticeable in the 3/4 views where cranial depth variation is more apparent. Frontal views show minimal change.
primary_face_region: upper face and cranium — forehead depth, temporal region, cranial vault anterior-posterior dimension

interactions.beta11: beta11 adjusts forehead height/roundness; beta15 adjusts forehead depth — complementary upper-face controls
interactions.beta1: beta1 is vertical stretch including forehead; beta15 is anterior-posterior depth — orthogonal dimensions

prose: Beta15 is a subtle cranial depth component that adjusts the anterior-posterior dimension of the upper skull. Positive values push the forehead forward and deepen the cranial vault, creating a slightly more prominent brow shelf and fuller temples. Negative values flatten the forehead and create a shallower cranial profile. The effect is most visible in 3/4 views where the depth dimension is more apparent; from the front, the change is barely perceptible. The component is noted in the CLAUDE.md spec as part of the stature axis, but the current codebase does not include a stature axis in the recipe — it uses only dominance for shape. The component is bilaterally symmetric and artifact-free throughout its range.
